subject: LU-7659 mdc: expose changelog through char devices

values.-1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

Patch Set 3:

Henri, at one time we discussed using truncate to drop old records instead of the "magic write". Using truncate() wasn't wuite the correct interface since that is to drop the end of the file. What is better is to allow fallocate(FALLOC_FL_PUNCH_HOLE, start, end) to drop the records in the specified range.

Patch Set 3:

Thanks Andreas, I'll do the change.

BTW, this patch has know issues, especially WRT multiple mount points. Next version will be more robust but I'm still not quite sure how to best make the link between an MDC obd_device and the associated char device (I'm currently registering one per MDT, not one per MDC)...

Any feedback would be much appreciated

Patch Set 3:

It is important to understand what the security requirements are for this. Is this exporting the ChangeLog to all clients?  Is there some way to restrict this to only a small number of clients (e.g. like root squash)?

If this is to be exposed on clients, what about exposing this interface via $MOUNT/.lustre/changelog/MDTnnnn?  If on the server, I guess this file could be in the MDT mountpoint?

Patch Set 3:

Also, what are the API/ABI compatibility implications of this change?  It appears that the llpi handling of this is maintained, but should the old ioctl interfaces be kept for some releases so that older versions of userspace tools will continue to work, or is the older RBH changelog processing tool dynamically linked to liblustreapi and this change is transparent?

Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1

(34 comments)

(Partial review. Will need to see it again when comments are addressed.)

I see memory leaks when running this.

 
export PTLDEBUG=malloc
export DEBUG_SIZE=128
llmount.sh
lctl --device lustre-MDT0000 changelog_register
touch /mnt/lustre/f{0..127}
lfs changelog lustre-MDT0000
lfs changelog_clear lustre-MDT0000 cl1 0
lfs changelog lustre-MDT0000
llmountcleanup.sh
perl ./lustre/tests/leak_finder.pl /tmp/debug-leak.1461173106
 
*** Two allocs with the same address (120 bytes at ffff88006e11ff20, mdc_request.c:chlg_en
queue_cb:2543)
*** Free different size (120 alloced, 96 freed).
    malloc at mdc_request.c:chlg_enqueue_cb:2543, free at mdc_request.c:chlg_read:2668

Patch Set 3:

(But do note that I am in favor of the approach here.)

Patch Set 3:

(30 comments)

Thanks a lot for the review!

Please find an updated version. It's still the initial design (exposed as character devices in /dev) but at least it should be a cleaner base for reviewers to think about or experiment with.

I have implemented and reverted the fallocate() section back, as it appears that the kernel won't invoke .fallocate handler for non-regular files (fail early with ENODEV)...

Patch Set 7:

Henri, what are your plans for this patch?  It would be great to get this included into the 2.10 release if possible, which is still accepting features for the next month or so.

Patch Set 7:

> Henri, what are your plans for this patch?  It would be great to
 > get this included into the 2.10 release if possible, which is still
 > accepting features for the next month or so.

The plan was to compare it to a netlink-based implementation but we don't have one yet. Let's give this patch a chance to land, I'll refresh and polish it.

Patch Set 8:

Robert, does Lemur or the CE copytool use the OBD_IOC_CHANGELOG_SEND or OBD_IOC_CHANGELOG_CLEAR ioctls directly, or does it go through the llapi_changelog_{recv,free,clear}() APIs?

This patch is improving the ChangeLog interface efficiency, so it would be good to land, but this will also break copytools that access the ioctl() interface directly.  Since this is first appearing in 2.10, it should give you time to add in support for the character device interface before this.

Patch Set 7:

Robert will confirm but it looks like the go-lustre bindings used by lemur call into llapi, so does robinhood indeed. The last user I could think of is Cray's new liblustre but it does not seem to have changelog-related functions implemented.

Patch Set 11:

(28 comments)

The big problem is that the current changelog API is not strictly defined and kept consistent.

I believe that this patch which introduces a clearer file semantic, will help.

I would like to add a real notification mechanism to llog, to prevent from having to scan it again and again when watching for changes. Once we have it, poll will make more sense and it will be possible to properly implement the behaviors described in changelog flags.

parents.subject: LU-9281 test: stop using invalid ps option

commit.subject: LU-7659 mdc: expose changelog through char devices
commit.message:
LU-7659 mdc: expose changelog through char devices

Register one character device per MDT in order to allow non-llapi to
read them and to make delivery more efficient.

- open() spawns a thread to prefetch records and enqueue them into a
  local buffer (unless the device is open in write-only mode).
- lseek() can be used to jump to a specific record, in which case the
  offset is a record number (with SEEK_SET) or a number of records to
  skip (SEEK_CUR). Movement can only be done forward.
- read() copies records to userland. No truncation happens, so short
  reads are likely.
- write() is used to transmit control commands to the device.
  The only available one is changelog_clear, which is done by writing
  "clear:cl<user>:<recno>" into the device.
- close() terminates the prefetch thread if any, and releases resources.

It is possible to poll() on the device to get notified when new records
are available for read.

Signed-off-by: Henri Doreau <henri.doreau@cea.fr>
Change-Id: I14709fdbac76b5512e58099e4e536cf9c973868c
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/18900
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <andreas.dilger@intel.com>
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: John L. Hammond <john.hammond@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
